title: Report and impact assessment on Copyright and Artificial Intelligence
description: A report and impact assessment on the use of copyright works in the development of artificial intelligence (AI) systems.
summary: This report assesses the impact of using copyrighted works in AI development, likely considering copyright infringement issues and potential solutions. It examines the intersection of intellectual property law and AI technology to determine the implications for copyright holders.

title: Artificial Intelligence and Public Standards: government response to report
description: The government's response to the Committee's 2020 report Artificial Intelligence and Public Standards
summary: The government has responded to a report on artificial intelligence and public standards by outlining its approach to regulating this technology. The response details how the government plans to implement the report's recommendations and advance the UK's use of AI while ensuring public safety and security.

title: Generative artificial intelligence (AI) in education
description: The Department for Education’s (DfE) position on the use of generative AI in the education sector.
summary: The Department for Education's policy on generative AI in education aims to provide guidance on its use in schools and universities. The policy seeks to ensure a responsible and controlled approach to generative AI, balancing its potential benefits with concerns about accuracy, bias, and academic integrity.

title: Copyright and artificial intelligence: progress report
description: A progress report on the use of copyright works in the development of AI systems, under Section 137 of the Data (Use and Access) Act.
summary: This report explores the use of copyrighted works in developing AI systems. It aims to provide a progress update under Section 137 of the Data (Use and Access) Act regarding this issue.

title: Transport artificial intelligence action plan
description: Sets out how DfT will work with the transport sector to exploit the opportunities from AI while managing potential risks.
summary: This is a plan created by the Department for Transport (DfT) to help the transport sector use artificial intelligence safely and effectively. It aims to balance the benefits and risks of AI in transportation.

title: The regulation of artificial intelligence as a medical device: government response to the RHC
description: Government response to the recommendations made by the Regulatory Horizons Council (RHC) on the regulation of AI as a medical device.
summary: The government is responding to recommendations on how to regulate the use of artificial intelligence in medical devices, such as diagnosing and treating patients. This response is focused on ensuring AI medical devices are safe and effective for use in healthcare.

title: ​​Ofqual’s approach to regulating the use of artificial intelligence in the qualifications sector​
description: Published in response to a request from the Secretary of State for Education and the Secretary of State for Science, Innovation and Technology.
summary: Ofqual has created a policy to regulate the use of artificial intelligence in qualifications, in response to a government request. The policy aims to ensure AI is used fairly and safely in the qualifications sector.

title: Government response to the House of Lords Select Committee on Artificial Intelligence
description: Government response to the Lords Select Committee on Artificial Intelligence report ‘AI in the UK: No Room for Complacency’.
summary: The UK government has issued a response to the House of Lords Select Committee on Artificial Intelligence, outlining its position on AI-related issues. The response addresses key concerns and recommendations from the committee's report, 'AI in the UK: No Room for Complacency'.

title: AI and Public Standards – Terms of Reference
description: Terms of Reference and FAQs for the Committee's review into artificial intelligence and public standards.
summary: The document outlines the terms of reference for a committee reviewing artificial intelligence and public standards, likely aiming to develop guidelines or recommendations. It also includes frequently asked questions (FAQs) to clarify the committee's scope and goals.

title: CSPL submission to Science and Technology Committee inquiry on Governance of AI
description: CSPL has submitted written evidence to the Science and Technology Committee's inquiry on Governance of Artificial Intelligence.
summary: The CSPL has submitted written evidence to an inquiry on the governance of Artificial Intelligence to the Science and Technology Committee. This submission is likely aimed at providing guidance and recommendations for the regulation and management of AI.
